label: CPT Rafael Martinez — Army, Iraq (OIF), Burn Pits, Tinnitus

dd214.name: Martinez, Rafael J.
dd214.decorations: Bronze Star Medal, Army Commendation Medal (2), Iraq Campaign Medal with 3 Campaign Stars, Combat Action Badge, Global War on Terrorism Service Medal, National Defense Service Medal
dd214.dutyLocations: Fort Leonard Wood MO, Camp Liberty Iraq, FOB Hammer Iraq, Joint Base Lewis-McChord WA, Camp Arifjan Kuwait
dd214.remarks: Completed three deployments to Iraq (OIF 06-08, OIF 09-10, OIF 11). Served as route clearance platoon leader and company commander. Documented burn pit exposure at FOB Hammer and Camp Liberty per DoD Airborne Hazards Open Burn Pit Registry. Member of Burn Pit Registry.

vaRecords.provider: VA Puget Sound Health Care System
vaRecords.notes: Patient reports persistent lower back pain since 2009 IED blast. Ringing in both ears constant since Iraq deployments. Began after repeated exposure to IED blasts and heavy equipment. MRI lumbar spine ordered.

vaRecords.provider: VA Puget Sound Health Care System
vaRecords.notes: MRI confirms L4-L5 disc herniation with mild nerve root impingement. Consistent with blast injury mechanism described by patient. Physical therapy referral placed. Patient reports pain at 6/10 on average, 8/10 on bad days.

Patient screened positive on PCL-5 (score: 58). Reports nightmares 3-4x/week, hypervigilance, avoidance of crowded places, difficulty concentrating. Onset traced to 2007 deployment when his vehicle was hit by an IED killing one soldier. Also reports persistent low mood, loss of interest, sleep disturbance. Started on sertraline 50mg and referred to PTSD clinical team.

Pure tone audiometry shows mild-to-moderate sensorineural hearing loss bilaterally, worse at 4000-6000 Hz. Pattern consistent with noise-induced hearing loss. Patient's MOS (12B Combat Engineer) involved repeated exposure to explosions, heavy machinery, and weapons fire. Hearing aids recommended. Tinnitus rated as constant, moderate severity, significantly impacts sleep.

diagnosis: Mild restrictive lung defect — pending further evaluation
vaRecords.notes: Patient reports chronic sinus congestion and intermittent shortness of breath since Iraq service. Documented burn pit exposure at FOB Hammer (2007-2008) and Camp Liberty (2009-2010). PFT shows mild restrictive pattern. CT sinuses shows mucosal thickening consistent with chronic sinusitis. Patient enrolled in VA Airborne Hazards and Open Burn Pit Registry.

privateRecords.provider: Dr. Sarah Chen, Tacoma Orthopedic Associates
privateRecords.notes: Patient referred for second opinion on back pain management. Concurs with VA diagnosis. Also notes left knee crepitus and reduced ROM — patient attributes to years of carrying heavy equipment and route clearance operations. X-ray shows early degenerative changes. Recommends continued PT and possible epidural injection for back.

privateRecords.provider: Dr. James Whitfield, Pacific Northwest Psychiatry
diagnosis: Major Depressive Disorder secondary to PTSD (F33.1)
privateRecords.notes: Independent medical opinion requested for VA disability claim. It is my professional opinion, stated to a reasonable degree of medical certainty, that CPT Martinez's PTSD is directly caused by his combat service in Iraq, specifically the IED attack of March 2007 and sustained combat operations over three deployments. His depression is at least as likely as not secondary to his PTSD. His insomnia is a direct symptom of both conditions. Current GAF: 48.

conditions: Chronic low back pain / L4-L5 disc herniation
Mild restrictive lung defect (burn pit related)
presumptiveExposures: Burn pits (FOB Hammer, Camp Liberty — Iraq)
summary.functionalImpact: Patient reports difficulty sitting or standing for more than 30 minutes. Ringing in ears disrupts sleep and concentration. Avoids crowds and loud environments. Nightmares cause sleep disruption 3-4 nights per week. States he had to leave a management position in 2020 because he could not handle the stress and concentration demands.
